topic: I replaced my $80k VA with AI agents. Here's the math.
hook_summary: I fired my VA and saved $60k. Here's how.

scenes.audio_text: Everyone says hire a VA before you're ready. I did that, spent $80k in a year. Then I replaced her with three AI agents for $300 a month.
scenes.visual_prompt: A 28-year-old man with short dark wavy hair and light stubble, wearing a dark polo shirt, sits at a wooden desk in a bright home office with natural light from a window. Medium close-up, locked-off camera, direct to lens. Candid documentary style.

scenes.audio_text: One handles inbox triage, another books client calls, the third drafts proposals. They don't sleep, don't complain, and cost less than my coffee budget.
scenes.visual_prompt: Same man now walking down a busy city street, holding a phone to his ear, slight smile. Over-shoulder shot, handheld motion, afternoon golden hour light. Photo-real.

scenes.audio_text: The hard truth? Most tasks you delegate can be automated. Stop hiring people to do what code can do. That's the math.
scenes.visual_prompt: Same man leaning against a brick wall in a quiet alley, arms crossed, looking directly at camera. Medium close-up, slow push-in, warm ambient light. Candid.
